topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my phone screen, a familiar chime that usually signaled a new assignment or a group chat update. But this time, the profile picture attached to the message made my breath hitch. It was Mr. Harrison, my notoriously stern history teacher, looking… relaxed. A picture of him smiling, leaning against what looked like a beach. My fingers hovered over the screen, my thumb trembling slightly. I quickly minimized the app, my heart beginning a frantic, uneven rhythm against my ribs. Why was he tagged in a photo that looked so casual? I navigated back to the platform, my eyes scanning the comments. Nothing overtly suspicious, just a few well-wishes. A cold knot tightened in my stomach as I realized the picture was clearly geotagged to a place I recognized as a private coastal retreat. I needed to check my settings, to see if this was some kind of elaborate prank, but the sheer incongruity of the image made my hands shake too much to type.

[story 2]

I was scrolling through Instagram during my lunch break, mindlessly flicking through posts, when a familiar handle popped up in my feed: @MrHarrisonHistory. I froze, the lukewarm cafeteria pizza suddenly tasting like ash in my mouth. It was him. My teacher. His profile was updated, and the latest post was a candid shot of him laughing with a group of people in what appeared to be a lively urban setting. My jaw tightened. I instinctively pulled my phone away from my lap, my knuckles turning white where I gripped the casing. A sudden flush crept up my neck, and I started scanning the room around me, half-expecting someone to notice the sudden stillness radiating off me. This wasn't normal. He wasn't just posting about school events; this felt invasive, a breach of the professional boundary I thought existed between us. I quickly switched to a different application, trying to rationalize the strange sensation of rapid pulse pounding in my ears.

[story 3]

It was late, the house quiet save for the hum of my computer. I was doing a final review of notes when a notification flashed across my screen, startling me into dropping my pen. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. The message contained only a cryptic emoji and a single word: "See you soon." My gaze darted around the room, the shadows seeming to deepen as my focus narrowed entirely on the text. My skin prickled, a distinct sensation of being watched, even though I was alone. I reread the message three times, trying to decipher any hidden meaning, any context that would make sense of the sudden, unsettling intimacy of the communication. A wave of intense unease washed over me, making my throat feel suddenly dry. I felt a desperate urge to close the laptop and retreat to my room, to physically distance myself from the digital intrusion that had just invaded my private space.
